q: Plants make their own organic molecules, using the process of ___.
Carbon dioxide is the source of ___ and light is the source of energy.
The main photosynthetic organs are the ___.
Leaves have evolved a large surface area to ___ ratio for efficient capturing of carbon dioxide and light.
Most plants do not have compact bodies like animals, but have extensive ___ bodies with leaves above the ground.
Plants have extensive ___ systems below the ground to obtain the water and mineral salts they need.
Water and mineral ions are absorbed by roots and transported through the ___ to other parts of the plant.
Glucose can be moved through the ___ as part of the sucrose molecule.
Sugars usually change into the form of ___, for storage in a potato tuber.
Plants can rely on ___ to supply their cells with oxygen and to remove carbon dioxide.
The leaves are very thin and have a large ___ area inside them in contact with the air spaces.
Xylem transports water and ___ from the roots to all parts of the plant.
Phloem transports substances made in the plant, such as ___ and amino acids.
Xylem tissue contains dead, empty hollow cells with no ___.
Xylem vessels are arranged in long lines to form a ___.
Phloem tissue contains cells called ___.
Unlike xylem vessel, sieve tubes are ___ cells and contain cytoplasm.
Sieve tube walls are made of ___.
A ___ is associated with each sieve tube.
Within a plant, ___ and organic compounds are transported by being dissolved in water.
The dissolved mineral ions are transported in the ___ tissue.
The dissolved organic compounds are transported in the ___ tissue.
The uptake of water is a ___ process and occurs by osmosis.
Osmosis is the diffusion of water from a higher water potential to a ___ water potential.
In ___ absorption, the water first enters the cell sap and passes from one cell to another.
Movement where protoplasm is involved is called ___.
In passive absorption, water moves through the ___ of the root.
The apoplast path includes the cell wall and ___ spaces.
Most water travels via the ___ pathway when transpiration rates are high.
In the apoplastic pathway, water moves by ___ as it does not pass through a partially permeable membrane.
When water reaches the endodermis, the presence of a thick, waterproof, waxy band of ___ blocks the apoplastic pathway.
The band of suberin is called the ___ and forms an impassable barrier for the water.
The movement of water in the ___ pathway is slower than the apoplastic pathway.
In the symplastic pathway, water moves by ___ into the cell.
In the symplastic pathway, water moves between cells through the ___.
The walls of the ___ cells are wet and some of this water evaporates into the air space.
The air in the internal spaces of the leaf contacted with the air outside through small pores called ___.
Transpiration is the process where water eventually evaporates and ___ out of the leaf.
If the water potential gradient between the air spaces in the leaf and the air outside becomes steeper, the rate of transpiration will ___.
In condition of ___ humidity, the gradient is steep, so transpiration takes place more quickly.
Transpiration rate may also be increased by an increasing in ___ or rising in temperature.
In most plants, stomata ___ during the day and close at night.
Stomata must be opened during the day to allow ___ to diffuse into the leaf for photosynthesis.
In hot conditions, transpiration plays an important role in ___ the leaves.
If water loss by transpiration exceeds the rate of uptake, the cells become ___ and the plant wilts.
Ascent of sap mostly occurs due to ___ exerted by transpiration of water.
Radiant heat falling on plants increases their temperature; transpiration by evaporating water can bring down their temperature by $10^{\circ}-15^{\circ}C$.
The development of ___ tissue is essential for providing rigidity and strength to the plant.
Mineral salts are mostly distributed by ___ column of sap.
The loss of water through transpiration increases the ___ of mineral salts in the plant.
Transpiration helps in better development of ___ system.
The ash and sugar content of the fruit ___ with the increase in transpiration.
Excessive transpiration induces ___ and resistant to moderate drought.
Transpiration helps the shape and structure of plant parts by keeping cells ___.
Transpiration supplies ___ for photosynthesis.
Loss of water from the twig produces a ___ in the thistle funnel and water will be drawn up.
___ is the movement of dissolved substances through a plant.
Carbohydrates synthesized in the leaves pass downwards or upwards in the ___ of the phloem.
The phloem tissue is a ___ tissue.
Sieve tubes or sieve cells have no ___ and companion cells possess do work together.
Sieve tubes are cylindrical cells with layers of ___ like materials just inside their cell walls.
The cell septate walls in phloem are known as ___ plates.
Sieve cells are kept alive by the nucleated ___ cells.
Phloem tissue is strengthened by ___ besides the sieve and companion cells.
Large multicellular animals have evolved transport system as organisms get larger and have higher ___ rates.
In many large animals, circulatory system is in the form of ___ system.
Single-celled organisms have a ___ surface area to volume ratio.
In large multicellular organisms, the surface area to volume ratio is ___.
Respiration supplies energy for life but also produces the toxic waste product ___.
Mass transport systems involve a system of ___ that carry substances.
A mechanical method of moving materials fast enough is the ___ of the heart.
A suitable transport medium in mass transport is usually a ___.
There are two main types of circulatory system: ___ circulatory system and closed circulatory system.
In an open circulatory system, blood pumped by the ___ heart leaves the open-ended vessels.
When the heart relaxes, the blood flows back into the vessels through pores called ___.
Open circulatory systems are found in most ___ such as insects, spiders and crabs.
In a closed circulatory system, blood is pumped by the heart into the ___.
Main advantages of a closed system are that ___ can be increased and flow can be directed precisely.
Single circulatory system is found in ___.
The fish heart pumps ___ blood to the gills.
In single circulatory systems, blood passes ___ through the heart.
Birds and mammals have evolved the most complex type of transport system known as a ___ circulatory system.
Double circulatory system involves two separate ___.
The ___ circulation carries oxygenated blood from the heart to the body cells.
The ___ circulation carries deoxygenated blood from the heart to the lungs.
Birds and mammals maintain a ___ body temperature.
Double circulation ensure that the oxygenated and deoxygenated blood cannot ___.
The cardiovascular system is made up of the heart, a series of blood vessels and the ___.
The passage of blood through the vessels is called the ___.
The human heart is a muscular pump with ___ chambers.
The upper two chambers of the heart are called the right atrium and the ___ atrium.
The lower chambers of the heart are the right ventricle and the ___ ventricle.
The walls of the ___ are thinner than those of the ventricles.
The right atrium receives blood from the ___ which collects deoxygenated blood from the upper body.
The left atrium receives oxygenated blood from the lungs via ___ veins.
The heart is made of a unique type of muscle, known as ___ muscle.
Cardiac muscle has a good blood supply by the ___ arteries.
Cardiac muscle contains lots of ___, a respiratory pigment which has a stronger affinity for oxygen.
The muscular wall of the ___ ventricle is much thicker than that of the right.
There are four sets of valves in the heart; two sets of ___ valves and two sets of semilunar valves.
The right atrioventricular valve is called ___ valves.
The left atrioventricular valve is called ___ valve.
The flaps of atrioventricular valves are supported by the ___ which prevent them from turning inside out.
___ valves are pocket-like valves with half-moon shaped like those in veins.
___ carry blood away from the heart towards the cells of the body.
The ___ carries deoxygenated blood from the heart to the lungs.
The ___ carries deoxygenated blood from the foetus to the placenta.
The ___ (large artery) leaving the heart branches off into arteries in every direction.
The very smallest branches of the arterial system are the ___.
Arterial walls contain a lot of ___ fibers so they can stretch to accommodate pressure surges.
The elastic return of fibers to original length after a surge is called ___.
___ carry blood back towards the heart.
The ___ carries oxygen-rich blood from the lungs back to the heart.
Tiny ___ lead from the capillary network, combining into larger vessels called veins.
There are one-way ___ at frequent intervals throughout the venous system.
___ have a very simple structure which is well adapted to their function.
Capillary walls consist of just one very thin ___.
Blood is composed of the ___, blood cells, and platelets.
___ is the liquid part of the blood.
Over 50% of the blood volume in the body is ___.
There are approximately 5 million ___ per $mm^{3}$ of blood.
Erythrocytes contain ___, a red pigment that carries oxygen.
Mature erythrocytes do not contain a ___ and have a limited life of about 120 days.
Erythrocytes have a ___ disc shape.
___ or white blood cells are much larger than erythrocytes but can squeeze through tiny blood vessels.
Leucocytes are made in the bone marrow, although some mature in the ___ gland.
Platelets are tiny fragments of large cells called ___.
___ causes the smooth muscle of the blood vessel to contract, narrowing the vessel.
___ is an enzyme that starts a sequence of chemical changes that clot the blood.
Thromboplastin catalyses the conversion of prothrombin into ___.
Thrombin acts on fibrinogen, converting it to an insoluble substance called ___.
Vitamin ___ is important in the production of many of the compounds needed for blood to clot.
Contractions of the heart are called ___.
The relaxation stage of the cardiac cycle is called ___.
Cardiac muscle cells are ___, which means they contract without any external stimulus.
The ___ (SAN) acts as the heart's own natural pacemaker.
Excitation spreads to another area of similar tissue called the ___ (AVN).
The ___ splits into two branches and carries the wave of excitation to the Purkyne tissue.
Between the capillaries and the cells is a watery liquid called ___ fluid.
Most of the water from tissue fluid reenters capillaries by ___.
The ___ system consists of vessels similar to blood capillaries, which are sometimes called lymphatic capillaries.
The ___ carry fats from villi of the small intestine.
Swellings called ___ filter dead cells and bacteria before lymph passes back into the blood.
